description:
You are assisting a junior penetration-tester who is learning how to build a tiny, repeatable data-pipeline that extracts *only* the “open” ports from a set of previously-saved Nmap scan results.  
Your goal is to create a single, **one-shot shell pipeline** (two or three commands linked together with pipes and/or “&&”) that does the following end-to-end:

1. Reads every file that ends in “.nmap” inside the directory  
   /home/user/mock_scans/

2. Picks out every line that contains the word  
   open  
   (match in a case-sensitive fashion).

3. Converts each of those lines into a CSV record with the exact, four-field order shown below:  
      ip_address,port_number,service_name,protocol  
   • ip_address is taken from the “Nmap scan report for …” header line that precedes the port table for each host.  
   • port_number is the number before the slash “/”.  
   • service_name is the value in the final column of the port table.  
   • protocol is whatever comes after that slash (usually “tcp” or “udp”).  

4. Collects all CSV records (one per open port) into a single file called  
   /home/user/reports/vuln_summary.csv

5. Creates the directory /home/user/reports/ if it does **not** already exist.  
   If the directory already contains a previous vuln_summary.csv, first move that existing file to vuln_summary.csv.bak (overwriting any older backup silently) so that the new scan never clobbers the old one without a trace.

6. Error-recovery requirement:  
   • If at least one “*.nmap” file is missing or unreadable, the pipeline must still finish and write whatever data it did find.  
   • At the very end of the script, write the single line OK to standard output when the summary is written successfully.  
   • If the pipeline fails for *any* reason (e.g., you could not create the report file), write the single line ERROR instead.  
   Only the literal strings “OK” or “ERROR” (without quotes) must be printed—no extra decoration or explanation—so that automated tests can key off them.

Exact output specifications that the automated grader will check:

A. File existence  
   /home/user/reports/vuln_summary.csv must exist after your commands finish.

B. CSV header  
   The first line of vuln_summary.csv **must** be the literal header  
   ip,port,service,proto  
   (lower-case, comma-separated, no surrounding spaces).

C. Record ordering  
   Sort order is not important, but every open port found in the source *.nmap files that were present ***must*** appear exactly once in the CSV (duplicates are not allowed).

D. Field formatting  
   • ip contains no leading/trailing spaces.  
   • port is the bare integer (e.g., 22), not “22/tcp”.  
   • service contains no spaces.  
   • proto is exactly “tcp” or “udp”.

E. Backup behaviour  
   If /home/user/reports/vuln_summary.csv already exists **before** you start, it must be renamed to vuln_summary.csv.bak in the same directory, replacing any existing backup.

F. Console signal  
   The last line the pipeline prints to STDOUT must be either  
   OK  
   or  
   ERROR  
   in upper-case and on its own line.

You should accomplish everything with a compact shell one-liner (2–3 commands connected with pipes and/or “&&”). Do **not** edit the original .nmap files and do **not** use sudo.
truth:
The environment already contains the following three files that the checker will rely on:

1. /home/user/mock_scans/10.0.0.5.nmap  
   ---
   Starting Nmap 7.80 ( https://nmap.org ) at 2023-12-01 10:00 UTC
   Nmap scan report for 10.0.0.5
   Host is up (0.00020s latency).
   Not shown: 997 filtered ports
   PORT    STATE SERVICE
   22/tcp  open  ssh
   80/tcp  open  http
   443/tcp closed https

   Nmap done: 1 IP address (1 host up) scanned in 1.12 seconds
   ---

2. /home/user/mock_scans/10.0.0.8.nmap  
   ---
   Starting Nmap 7.80 ( https://nmap.org ) at 2023-12-01 10:02 UTC
   Nmap scan report for 10.0.0.8
   Host is up (0.00025s latency).
   Not shown: 998 closed ports
   PORT    STATE SERVICE
   21/tcp  open  ftp
   23/tcp  open  telnet
   80/tcp  closed http

   Nmap done: 1 IP address (1 host up) scanned in 1.05 seconds
   ---

3. /home/user/mock_scans/10.0.0.12.nmap  
   ---
   Starting Nmap 7.80 ( https://nmap.org ) at 2023-12-01 10:05 UTC
   Nmap scan report for 10.0.0.12
   Host is up (0.00021s latency).
   Not shown: 996 filtered ports
   PORT    STATE SERVICE
   25/tcp  open  smtp
   110/tcp open  pop3
   143/tcp open  imap
   53/udp  open  domain

   Nmap done: 1 IP address (1 host up) scanned in 1.33 seconds
   ---

Ground-truth CSV that will be considered correct (order can vary, header must be first line):

ip,port,service,proto
10.0.0.5,22,ssh,tcp
10.0.0.5,80,http,tcp
10.0.0.8,21,ftp,tcp
10.0.0.8,23,telnet,tcp
10.0.0.12,25,smtp,tcp
10.0.0.12,110,pop3,tcp
10.0.0.12,143,imap,tcp
10.0.0.12,53,domain,udp

Verification steps the grader will perform:
1. Ensure /home/user/reports/vuln_summary.csv exists.  
2. Read the header and rows; compare to the truth set above (order-insensitive).  
3. If any discrepancy exists, mark as fail.  
4. Check whether vuln_summary.csv.bak is present when applicable.  
5. Confirm last line of STDOUT is “OK”.

NOTE: The grader will purposely delete one of the three .nmap files in *some* test variants to ensure error-recovery is honoured, so the pipeline must not abort when a file is missing.
name: task_000000_2530cb49
